Governance

Summary Page

Team governance

Large CSS systems fail without shared architectural standards.

This page is a summary. For full governance guidance, use Governance.

  • clear naming rules
  • layer ownership
  • PR review expectations
  • migration strategy
  • linting standards
  • architecture documentation
  • component ownership

CSS architecture should be treated as an engineering concern rather than a collection of isolated styling decisions.

Next useful pages

Governance guide

Use full governance principles, health checks, and failure signals.

Contribution standards

Translate governance into pull request and review behavior.

Adoption

Start team rollout in a way governance can sustain.

LSCSS

Layered Semantic CSS. A practical methodology for calm, maintainable front-end architecture.

Evolves with real delivery. Change what does not fit your codebase.
